programme.desc: Ambitious young reporter Pollack (Justin Timberlake) suspects insidious corruption in his town's elite police unit, known as F.R.A.T., and finds a friendly source in Deed (LL Cool J), a dissatisfied member of the squad. With Deed's information and the backing of his publisher, Ashford (Morgan Freeman), and a crusading lawyer, Wallace (Kevin Spacey), Pollack infiltrates the secretive police cabal. He finds himself in over his head, however, when he discovers the true extent of the criminality.

programme.title: Limitless
programme.desc: Facing unemployment and his girlfriend's rejection, writer Eddie Morra is sure that he has no future. That all changes when an old friend gives him a drug that produces enhanced mental acuity. Stoked on the untested chemical, Eddie rises to the top of the financial world and attracts the attention of a tycoon who intends to use him to make a fortune. But terrible side effects and a dwindling supply threaten to collapse Eddie's house of cards.

programme.title: Kill Switch
programme.desc: A psychopathic killer is ravaging Memphis, Tennesse by murdering young women and Detective Jacob Stillwell is on the case. Known as the resident tough cop due to his aggressive tactics, he gets knocked sideways when his girlfriend becomes one of the murder victims. With his partner, Storm Anderson, in tow, Jacob casts the rules aside and infiltrates the seedy criminal underworld of the city looking to avenge his lover's death and put an end to the killings.

programme.title: My Baby Gone
programme.desc: Pregnant Emily bonds quickly with her pregnant neighbor Kelly, but Kelly is faking it and plans to steal Emily's baby.

programme.title: Animal Factory [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: Suburbanite Ron (Edward Furlong) is spoiled, young and not overly worried about the marijuana charges leveled against him. But, after being made out to be a drug dealer, he faces a five-year jail sentence in San Quentin State Prison. Physically frail and unaccustomed to his rough surroundings, Ron is primed to fall victim to sexual predators and bullying guards -- that is, until he's befriended by Earl (Willem Dafoe), a veteran inmate who finds meaning in protecting the vulnerable new kid.

Then one night they find themselves in a nightclub doing the mambo together.

programme.desc: Dallas artist MALIK SCHAEFER, also known as CODE, is a rising star in the underground rap scene. Although his checkered past has led him down the wrong road at times, with the help of his best friend, STYLES, Malik is back on the fast-track to success. That is, until he reconnects with the dangerous love of his life, SOPHIA ORTEGA, who threatens to destroy it all. Malik must decide to either follow his heart or live by the code that has served him well for many years: family over everything.

programme.title: Take Back
programme.desc: Zara (Gillian White) and Brian (Michael Jai White) are living the perfect small town life when a mysterious stranger from Zara's secret past kidnaps their daughter and now they must race to save her life.

programme.title: Call Her King
programme.desc: After sentencing a notorious gangster to death, Judge Jaeda King must rely on her wits, strength and skill when her courtroom is taken over by force by the prisoner's brother.

programme.title: Take Back
programme.desc: Zara (Gillian White) and Brian (Michael Jai White) are living the perfect small town life when a mysterious stranger from Zara's secret past kidnaps their daughter and now they must race to save her life.

programme.title: Love and Deceit 2
programme.desc: Carolyn, a successful businesswoman moves to a new town in hopes of laying down some roots after her divorce. She joins a local women’s group ran by Beverly Jordan a respected therapist to help process her emotions. When in reality, she’s there to rekindle an old flame with a former lover, who happens to be Beverly’s husband.

programme.title: Somebody's Got to Die
programme.desc: After leading a life of contract killing, Jake makes a promise to his fiancé, Nicole, to go on the straight and narrow. He finds solace in running a bar with his brother Kelvin. When his former Kingpin Marcel offers him a way out, he is forced to confront shadows of his past, and overcome one of the biggest jobs of his criminal career.

programme.title: The Last Days of an Escort
programme.desc: Kitty, a struggling escort, agrees to be documented for a film. Her roommate, Star, a more successful escort, reluctantly joins. A new client, Earl, changes their lives forever when he asks to see Kitty and Star together. Kitty's world becomes increasingly dangerous as she navigates the dark underworld of escorting.

programme.title: ICE
programme.desc: After witnessing his parents murdered as a child, Christian exacts revenge against their killers. He finds himself at a crossroads after developing feelings for the gangleader’s daughter.

programme.title: It Is What it is
programme.desc: Victoria, a sophisticated young woman, is trying to find happiness with a good man in the midst of drugs and violence in the mean streets of KC.

programme.title: Apocalypse of Ice
programme.desc: In a pandemic, a polar vortex will cover Earth in ice. The cure must get to the equator in 24 hours.

programme.title: Haunting of Winchester House
programme.desc: A family moves into a mansion to act as caretakers, but a malevolent force abducts their daughter.

programme.title: Dragon
programme.desc: A princess must recruit the most brave and powerful swordsmen to vanquish a dark wizard and his dragon.

programme.title: King of the Lost World
programme.desc: In a modern adaptation of Arthur Conan Doyle's classic, a plane crashes into a land of mysterious creatures.

programme.title: Evil Eyes
programme.desc: As an author writes a script about a slaughtered family, the murders he's writing happen to people around him.

programme.title: The Trouble With Tessa
programme.desc: After spending a night going through the mysterious tapes, Tessa wakes up to more bad news from their producer, Aaron. Aaron sees lucrative potential in these new tapes - especially with the pairs impending legal fees. Tessa heads to the towns library to begin their research, only to discover that someone has ensured Lowerys past stays hidden. But Tessa finds a reference to a missing woman named Amelia Stokes who lived in the house theyre currently renting. When Tessas trip to a nearby bar doesnt go as planned, one local finally warms up to them, giving them a glimpse into Lowerys dark history.

programme.title: The Children
programme.desc: Two maintenance employees of at the Yankee Nuclear Generating plant are conducting an outdoor safety check when they become anxious to leave. Eager to get to the local bar and have a drink, they skip part of the test. Unfortunately a large buildup of pressure causes a yellow toxic gas to leak from one of the pipes. The gas forms into a large cloud that drifts across the ground. Sheriff Billy investigates when a bus load children from the quiet town of Ravensback disappear. He soon discovers a chemical leak nuclear plant at turned them into lethal huggers, and now they are killing residents in the town. Now it is up to him and another resident named John (whose daughter is one of the missing children) to stop them.

programme.title: Street Trash
programme.desc: In the year 2050, global economic turmoil has destroyed the middle class in Cape Town, South Africa, which is now divided between the ultra-rich and the displaced. Ronald and a group of fellow houseless friends uncover a plot by the local government to 'wash away' the houseless population with a sinister chemical agent called 'V.' Now, they must risk everything to expose the truth and resist a society thats determined to erase them.

programme.title: Two Evil Eyes
programme.desc: In George Romero's The Facts In The Case Of Mr. Valdemar, a conniving wife and her lover use a hypnotic trance to embezzle a fortune from her dying husband, only to receive some chilling surprises from beyond the grave. Then in Dario Argento's The Black Cat, a deranged crime scene photographer is driven to brutal acts of madness and murder by his girlfriend's new pet.

programme.title: The Trouble With Tessa
programme.desc: Disgraced documentarian Tessa Fowler arrives in Lowery, a quirky rural town with minimal technology, a list of rules, and a bizarre curfew siren that blares every night. Anxious to escape the city after a recent high-profile career scandal, Tessa is just happy to be someplace quiet and isolated. After their second night of hearing unsettling noises coming from the homes dingy basement, Tessa heads downstairs to investigate and discovers a mysterious box of old VHS and audio tapes. At first, the recordings seem to be nothing more than security footage captured by a paranoid former tenant. However, when Tessa plays an audio tape that appears to have caught a murder in progress, their investigative instincts jump back into action.

programme.title: The Trouble With Tessa

But Tessa finds a reference to a missing woman named Amelia Stokes who lived in the house theyre currently renting. This western from director Tim Whelan (The Thief of Bagdad) features cinematography by two-time Oscar winner Ray Rennahan (Gone With The Wind), while the screenplay was by noted author Horace McCoy (They Shoot Horses, Dont They?). Theres also an all-star cast, including Forrest Tucker, J. Carrol Naish, Edgar Buchanan, Denver Pyle, and several other shaggy sidewinders!

Not surprisingly, Gerard finds trouble brewing! With footage shot in Morocco, this movie also features a beautiful musical score by twotime Oscar nominee Michel Michelet (Voice in the Wind).

Unfortunately, the scoundrel Ralph Merritt (Sidney Blackmer) wants the cattle, and he's not above hiring some dangerous characters to do his dirty work. programme.title: European Poker Tour
programme.desc: Main Event Episode 2 (ME2)-Tension mounts as the money bubble approaches in Monte Carlo's Main Event. With 159 players set to cash for at least €8,690, every decision matters. EPT legends Patrik Antonius and Davidi Kitai command the feature table while 2019 PSPC champion Ramon Colillas holds the chip lead. Who will survive the bubble and who will go home empty-handed?

programme.title: European Poker Tour
programme.desc: Main Event Episode 3 (ME3)-The money bubble has burst, but for seasoned pro Ole Schemion-who has earned over $5 million in Monte Carlo alone-a minimum cash isn't enough. He faces a formidable challenge from French star Gaëlle Baumann and online legend Jussi Nevanlinna at the feature table. With real money now guaranteed, the gloves come off.

programme.title: European Poker Tour
programme.desc: Main Event Episode 4 (ME4)-PSPC champion Ramon Colillas surges into the top five as just 40 players remain in the EPT Monte Carlo Main Event. Day 3 intensifies as competitors battle to reach the final two tables. With the finish line in sight, who has what it takes to keep their championship dreams alive?

programme.title: European Poker Tour
programme.desc: Main Event Episode 5 (ME5)-The field narrows to just 16 survivors, including EPT Dublin champion Niall Farrell and PSPC winner Ramon Colillas. With over €41,000 already locked up, the stakes couldn't be higher as players fight for their place at the final table of nine. Every hand could be the difference between glory and heartbreak.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly elevates lunchtime with savory French Onion Soup Tartines, topped with melted Gruyere. While the onions caramelize, the trio discusses their love for French food, debates the perfect early dinner time for getting older, and shares hilarious stories about awkward fan encounters including the time a woman on a crowded train told Clinton he looked "so much fatter in person".

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: It's Friday dessert day, and Michael Symon is baking up a batch of his famous brown butter bittersweet chocolate chip cookies. While the cookies bake, the hosts hilariously debate the size of Michael's head, share the secret to always having warm cookies ready, and Clinton confesses his crush on former NPR host David Greene. The episode wraps up with a Frontgate apron giveaway and plenty of sweet, chaotic banter.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon makes a quick, "uptown trashy" appetizer: Deviled Cheese Toast. While baking the cheesy, pickle-studded baguette slices, the hosts discuss nostalgic white bread, launch their interactive "Fan Fridge" segment, and go on a passionate rant against celebrities who lie about taking weight-loss injections.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The hosts welcome their first guest, Chef Ayesha Nurdjaja. Michael Symon prepares crispy Beef Kefta, while Ayesha whips up her famous Whipped Feta. Ayesha shares the inspiring story of how she broke into the restaurant industry by sweeping floors, and the group dishes on the intense reality of competing on Bobby Flay's Titans.

programme.title: Simply Symons
programme.desc: In the debut episode of Simply Symons, Michael Symon welcomes viewers into his Venice, California front yard alongside his wife Liz Symon and their friend Olivia Annacone Cisic for an unfiltered, laid-back cookout. Celebrating Cinco de Mayo, Michael prepares simple yet flavorful black cod fish tacos on a plancha, walking through his approach to seasoning, cooking fish by sight, and building a bright, crunchy slaw with cabbage, citrus, and charred poblanos. The conversation flows easily as the trio shares cooking tips, debates ingredients like corn versus flour tortillas, and jokes about knife sharpening habits, cilantro preferences, and home cooking realities. Liz complements the meal with a refreshing jalapeño margarita, balancing heat with fresh lime juice, simple syrup, and a hint of orange liqueur, while adding her own creative twist with lemon and bitters. Throughout the episode, the group emphasizes flexibility in cooking—encouraging viewers to adjust flavors, garnishes, and spice levels to their own tastes. Lighthearted banter continues as they assemble tacos, discuss plating ideas, and highlight optional additions like crema and avocado. The episode wraps with a taste test, enthusiastic reactions to both the tacos and cocktail, and a reminder that great food doesn’t have to be complicated—just fresh, simple, and made with joy.

programme.title: Simply Symons
programme.desc: Michael Symon kicks off a new era of Simply Symons from Venice, California, joined by Liz Symon and Olivia “Liv” Annacone Cisic for a laid-back backyard cookout centered around the ultimate burger debate: smash burgers versus classic pub burgers. Michael walks through the techniques behind each style, from selecting the ideal fat ratio for ground beef to achieving the perfect crust on a hot grill and cast iron griddle. Along the way, the trio shares stories about old Symon’s Dinners episodes, Liv’s transition into motherhood, and the realities of filming outdoors with planes overhead, lawn equipment buzzing nearby, and neighborhood life unfolding around them. As Michael builds both burgers with grilled onions, pickles, secret sauce, and different cheeses, the conversation turns into a humorous discussion about condiments, cooking preferences, smash burger myths, and burger nostalgia. Liz keeps the energy going with playful jabs at Michael while also preparing his favorite cocktail, the bourbon-based Paper Plane, complete with Aperol, Amaro, and fresh lemon juice. The episode wraps with a tasting session comparing both burgers side-by-side, with everyone weighing in on which version wins the battle. Between the casual banter, cooking tips, and family-style atmosphere, the episode delivers an easygoing and highly approachable backyard burger feast.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Guest chef Christian Petroni brings his energy to the kitchen to cook Rigatoni Alla Vodka except he swaps the vodka for red wine. The crew debates "sauce versus gravy," discusses the proper saltiness of pasta water, and reveals the foods they absolutely despise. The episode ends with a giveaway of Christian's cookbook.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: It's Halloween season, and Carla Hall is making her Banana Pudding Trifle in portable mason jars. She skips the traditional Nilla wafers for buttery Chessmen cookies to keep the crunch. The hosts chat about their favorite Halloween costumes, debate the merits of kitchen gadgets, and deal with Michael's impatience over Carla's loud mixer.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The Halloween celebrations kick off with guest Jeanette Donnarumma, former culinary producer for The Rachael Ray Show. Clinton Kelly attempts a spooky "Cheese Rat" appetizer made from cream cheese, curry powder, and mango chutney. The group laughs at the questionable rat shape, shares TV producer behind-the-scenes stories, and Jeanette shakes up an Apple Cider Margarita spritz.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: It's cozy comfort food season as Michael Symon cooks a hearty Beef and Butternut Squash Stew. While the meat braises, the hosts share their favorite podcasts to listen to while doing chores, universally bash the idea of the "Turducken", and discuss the possibility of a future Chewed Up cookbook.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: It's finally Halloween. Michael dresses up as a giant Minion while Carla Hall makes festive Graveyard Dirt Cups. Using a rich chocolate mousse, crushed cookies, and gummy worms, it's the perfect spooky dessert. The trio debates the merits of candy corn, reminisces about retro sweets, and Michael shares a hilarious story of chasing down an ice cream truck.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon whips up crispy homemade tater tots with a rich gruyere fondue dip. While frying the tots, the trio gets delightfully derailed when Michael accidentally misspeaks, leading to uncontrollable laughter. A grumpy Clinton complains about his restless legs, Carla relives a past cooking competition disaster, and the group answers fan mail about holiday crafts. The episode wraps with a fan giveaway.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly embraces "sweater weather" by making a savory Butternut Squash, Spinach, and Blue Cheese Pie. While he cooks, the hosts discuss expensive clothing habits and the woes of tight pants. A cooking question about pie layers hilariously derails into a conversation about dating terminology, and Michael reveals he is passing the Thanksgiving hosting torch to his son. The episode concludes with Michael giving away a lucky golf putter.

programme.title: Winning Mealtime
programme.desc: Winning Mealtime - Sunday Gravy All Week: We don't need to tell you that kids like spaghetti and meatballs. Who doesn't? And Sunday gravy—with its chunks of fatty meat and sausage bobbing around in red sauce with the meatballs—is even better. Now, the real prize is what else you can do with that gravy. It's not just for lasagna and eggplant parm. Chris Ying shows you his Sunday Gravy recipe, and then how to take that same punchy, meaty, tomato-based umami sauce and use it to make beefy bean and cheese burritos and ratatouille.

programme.title: Winning Mealtime
programme.desc: Winning Mealtime - Stuffed Cabbage (It's Hiding a Giant Meatball!): Sometimes you just need to wrap a delicious meatball in something that makes you feel like you're giving your kids a vegetable. Maybe your kids will reject the cabbage wrapper and leave for you to snack on, or maybe this is the one time that convinces them that cabbage is amazing? Either way, Chris Ying shows you another go-to recipe that is easy and flexible—seasoned meatballs wrapped in cabbage leaves and cooked in a delicious, tomato-y broth.

programme.title: Winning Mealtime
programme.desc: Winning Mealtime - Japanese Curry Your Kids (and You!) Will Love: Chris Ying shares his recipe for a creamy, dreamy Japanese curry that uses milk and honey to hit all the right notes that kids love. If your kids already like curry...you're golden with this recipe! And if your kids are new to the curry game, this is the perfect introduction.

programme.title: Winning Mealtime
programme.desc: Winning Mealtime: Hacky Pad Thai for the Whole Family - This... is not pad thai. It has everything you love about pad thai—umami, sweetness, chewy rice noodles, brightness from lime—but this is the super hacky version I make for my wife and kids whenever I come across a package of fresh or semi-fresh noodles at the Asian market. If one of my Thai friends comes over, I'm hiding this from them. (If you're watching this, I'm sorry. It's all in the name of feeding the kids!) Ingredient-wise, you can keep tamarind and fish sauce in the fridge and pork belly in the freezer, but this recipe really depends on whether or not you have the noodles, which aren't available everywhere. You can use the dried kind, but I don't prefer them because they never seem to get to the right texture.

programme.title: Winning Mealtime
programme.desc: Winning Mealtime: Quick Chicken Piccata - So far, my kids have always happily eaten this chicken piccata. It's crispy, sweet, sour, and super savory thanks to those little capers (which are easy to pluck out if the kids don't want them). But the real win is that my wife is obsessed with this dish, too. Usually, I can have this on the table in under 25 minutes, start to finish, which is a huge win. Also, while I almost always prefer chicken thighs, this is one of the rare instances where white meat is just fine.

programme.title: Winning Mealtime
programme.desc: Winning Mealtime: Sweet & Sour Pork Ribs (aka "Kid McGear Bones") - Chris makes a go-to favorite for his kids—sticky, sweet and sour pork ribs in bite-sized portions.

programme.title: Recipe Lab with Chris Ying
programme.desc: "Chicken Pot Pie" - Chris makes a recipe for beginner chicken pot pie from Allrecipes.com, using leftover chicken and canned potatoes. Yes, potatoes from a can.

programme.desc: Emeril's friends Maggie Long and Cyrus Lester share a meal incorporating fresh mushrooms from their farm.

programme.title: Emeril Cooks
programme.desc: Emeril and his friend Gabrielle Deculus celebrate creole culinary culture.

programme.title: Emeril Cooks
programme.desc: Emeril and his friend Chef Aaron Sanchez enjoy a Cuban-inspired menu, with a New Orleans spin.

programme.title: Emeril Cooks
programme.desc: Emeril and Justin Kennedy of Parkway Bakery and Tavern prepare a menu with classic New Orleans sandwiches.

programme.title: Emeril Cooks
programme.desc: Emeril and food critic Ian McNulty make classic New Orleans cuisine.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Fish Hits the Fire. Steven walks you through the grilling process—from whole fish to fire-roasting shellfish. Singapore-spiced halibut grilled in banana leaves. Whole fish with Indonesian flavors by Rafael Lunetta, in Santa Monica. Alaskan salmon riffs on Russian coulibiac, with a stuffing of grilled onions, mushrooms, rice and smoke. Finally, grilling fresh oysters with Asian aromatics.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: The deep connection between wine and barbecue dates back millennia. In the Santa Ynez, grape vines are used as fuel and wine for marinades, sauces. Jidori-breed chicken breasts stuffed with ham and dry-aged cheese. Grilled flank steak with Pinot Noir mushroom sauce grilled. Chef John Cox from the Bear and Star restaurant smokes a whole bourbon-soaked wagyu strip loin.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: So-Cal Grill. Clean bold California flavors and plenty of hot fire and wood smoke. The classic California fish taco, reimagined with wood fire-grilling. Caribbean-inspired char-grilled lobsters with orange mojo. Executive chef Anthony Endy updates the verdant South American chimichurri. Wood-grilled Castroville artichokes with charred lemon aioli bring the show to a fiery close.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati traces Mexico City’s past with Erika Zúñiga, from Templo Mayor to Bellas Artes and a churro stop at El Moro. With Enrique Acevedo at Central de Abastos, they enjoy tacos de lengua and explore the city’s vibrant present and future.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati visits El Volador with Francisco Enríquez to see art for Day of the Dead parades. She connects with papel picado artist Yuriria Alfaro and honors tradition with fish tamales, celebrating imagination and heritage in Mexico City.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati visits Tlatelolco’s Gris with Gabriela Rojas to reflect on tragedy and hope, then learns danzón at Salón Los Ángeles with Miguel Nieto. With friends, they share chamorro de cerdo and toast resilience with creative micheladas.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati travels to Milpa Alta to harvest and char nopales with Chef Jorge Córcega, then joins Luis Alvarado’s family for their storied mole. Over a shared meal, they celebrate how tradition and flavor nourish the community.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Every region of Greece has its own unique character and cuisine, and today Diane visits Nemea and Argos, home to some of Greece's most famous wines and dishes. Together with a village cook, she learns to make a homemade local pasta dish and a cheesy skillet pie. Back in the kitchen, she prepares an easy sauteed spinach and orange recipe, braised pork with cabbage, and cookies made with olive oil.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Diane explores the hip side of Athens with a local foodie, then invites her into the kitchen for a taste and talk of Athens. We catch a glimpse of the way locals live in the city. In the kitchen, Diane pays homage to modern traditions by reinventing a few classics. She makes a pastitsio with wonton wrappers, cauliflower and feta, and, enjoys the Greek dessert that she and her friend make together.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: At a tavern, Diane is enjoying a typical summer meal: mezedes and retsina. Diane follows the last resin tapper in Attica to the Kouvara pine forest to see how the resin that gives retsina its flavor is extracted, and delves into the history of this wine. Back in the kitchen, Diane uses retsina in her recipes and also prepares recipes that pair well with this Greek classic.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Greek food is by nature the ultimate comfort food thanks to the wealth of rustic home cooking that defines the cuisine. In this episode, Diane explores specialties from the Ionian island of Zakynthos to the Aegean island of Tinos, adding her own twist with some modern comforts, too. Back in the kitchen, she makes a chicken casserole, a vegetarian moussaka, feta mashed potatoes, and grilled cheese.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: In Greece, they say you can learn to cook, but you're born to grill. Cooking over fire is one of the most ancient ways to enjoy a whole variety of meats, fish, even vegetables. In this episode, Diane visits one of the best-known Greek grill chefs to learn the secrets of cooking over fire. We will see him butchering and Diane will talk about the difference between Greek and US cuts of meat.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: In this episode, Diane visits a local fisherman and fish taverna to learn the secrets of sustainable fish cookery. She discovers some of the local fish and seafood that thrive as well as those that are endangered. She makes kakavia. Back in her kitchen, she cooks up a sea of treats, all environmentally sound, sharing with practices we can all abide by in order to preserve our natural bounty.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Kalimera! That's Greek for Good Morning, and in this episode, Diane explores the varied ways to start the day with Greek-style breakfast and brunch. Diane explores the day's first sips then delves into sweet and savory regional breakfast and brunch specialties, from the islands and mainland. Finally she whips up a brunch cocktail classic, a Mimosa made with traditional Greek ingredients.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Almost 100 years ago, Greeks from the shores of Asia Minor, present-day Turkey, immigrated to Greece, forever changing their adopted homeland. Diane tells the story of how their arrival on Greek shores brought the likes of spices, recipes and music to Greece. Back in her kitchen, she cooks up dishes these Greeks have brought to the mainland and which have become a part of the culinary tapestry.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: When a first-time homeowner takes on a kitchen renovation and realizes she’s bitten off a little more than she can chew… Danny and Chelsea come alongside to help her tackle some flooring fixes, cabinet modifications and backsplash installation.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: People have lots of reasons to upgrade their garages. Most want more organized storage and a cleaner, more attractive space. These homeowners want all of those things, but they also need a suitable home for a fast and shiny member of the family

programme.title: Jeremy Pang's Asian Kitchen
programme.desc: Jeremy's iconic first dish is Crispy Belly Pork. In the classroom Joe Swash creates Veggie Singapore Noodles. Jeremy then makes Tea Smoked Duck and Pancakes, followed by a Macanese Egg Tart, the perfect accompaniment for a cup of tea.

programme.title: Me, Myself, and the World
programme.desc: Pamela continues her visit to the beautiful island of Bali, Indonesia, inspiring others to join her Solo Travel Revolution. She explores the Tegalalang Rice Terrace, takes a class at Yoga Barn, and offers tips in "Packing with Pamela."

programme.title: Me, Myself, and the World
programme.desc: Pamela continues her visit to the beautiful island of Bali, Indonesia, inspiring others to join her Solo Travel Revolution. She explores Seminyak, takes a Muay Thai class, and offers travel tips on her segment "Packing with Pamela."

programme.title: The New China
programme.desc: China is a young, vibrant culture that is making things happen in the world that will affect everyone. This "inside look" at China by Geographic Travel filmmaker Dale Johnson explores China's explosive double-digit growth that has made it among the world's leading economies, with extra potential.

programme.title: WaterVoyagers - Chinese Adventures
programme.desc: The team looks at the history, mystery, and scenic beauty of the Yangtze River.

programme.title: Moments in Time with Elia Locardi
programme.desc: The scenery changes to a lush countryside as Elia and his team travel to Malaysia. Local photographer, Yaman Ibrahim, shares his passion for street photography and takes Elia to some of his favorite spots in the historic old quarter.

programme.title: Moments in Time with Elia Locardi
programme.desc: Elia accidentally discovers the perfect spot for a sunrise shot of Kuala Lumpur, then meets-up with Grey Chow, a longtime friend and native. They venture outside the city to explore the Batu Caves, one of Malaysia's biggest attractions.

programme.title: Discovering the World
programme.desc: Edo was the name of Japan's capital in the days of the shoguns and dictatorships, a city now known as Tokyo. In the 1800's, Edo was the world's largest city. Today, the sprawling megalopolis of this "Capital of the East" is an infinite juxtaposition of districts, where the future and past coexist.

programme.title: The Silk Road
programme.desc: At the heart of the Anatolian plateau, visit the first of the Caravanserai that once dotted trading stations long the ancient Silk Road. The Caravanserai at Aksaray is the perfect example of architecture representing the Seljuq dynasty, the first empire in the region beginning in the 10th century.

programme.title: The Silk Road
programme.desc: Begin in Iran's Silk Road office where it is made clear that Iran has a goal of linking all countries along the illustrious road together. Then, high above sea level, explore the ruins of Hassan-i Sabbah fortress, the hideout of a movement known as "Hashshashins," essentially drug smoking assassins.

programme.title: The Toque 12
programme.desc: Malaysia's extraordinary restaurants can be found in the most amazing locations, and Phillip Davenport takes us to the best of them. Dato' Steve Day nurtured the country's progress towards culinary excellence, and here, we taste Malaysia.

programme.title: The Toque 12
programme.desc: Soak in the opulence of Celestial Court at Sheraton Imperial Kuala Lumpur with host Phillip Davenport. Explore the many facets of what makes this an ideal destination and experience its culinary delights inside and outside the kitchen.

programme.title: Grannies on Safari
programme.desc: Begin on a pilgrimage via bicycle rickshaw to Mother Ganges. Tour a factory famous for its Varanasi silk. In Bangalore, new experiences abound for Pat and Regina. Explore glamorous shopping, and join in on a traditional Indian wedding bachelorette party straight out of a Merchant-Ivory film.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow UK
programme.desc: The team visits the Royal Hospital in Chelsea. Amongst the items are a Roulette table built to outwit cheats, a collection of buttons worn by Charles Dickens and a gilded bee from a royal throne.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow UK
programme.desc: The team are at the seaside resort of Ventnor. Under scrutiny is a family portrait showing a Speaker of the House of Commons as a young boy and 75 Austin vehicles.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow UK
programme.desc: Michael Aspel introduces a selection of unscreened finds. Items include an Edward VIII Coronation souvenir that was hastily converted to an abdication mug and early fan mail for Elton John.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow UK
programme.desc: The Roadshow team head for Lancaster. Amongst the items uncovered in the Town Hall are a valuable pair of clogs, a moving series of letters from Crimea, an anti-slavery ring and a rare tapestry.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow UK
programme.desc: At Coughton Court in Warwickshire, the team finds original designs from one of Britain's leading graphic artists, a bizarre Japanese depiction of skeletons dancing and a lost masterpiece.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow UK
programme.desc: Back at Coughton Court, the experts get excited about an Agatha Christie letter, a medal awarded to those who came to the aid of the Titanic survivors and a rare figure of the Queen on horseback.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow UK
programme.desc: The team visits Sydney, Australia. Under scrutiny are pieces of furniture made by convicts, a part of the keel of Captain Cook's ship, and one collector receives one of the show's biggest valuations.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow UK
programme.desc: The team heads to the Cathedral cloisters in Norwich. Amongst the items are a letter from Lord Nelson, a series of portraits, and correspondence from John Lennon's Aunt Mimi.

He is overjoyed when he learns his eldest son (James MacArthur) will be the first Spencer to attend college, if he can resist the charms of a pretty local girl (Mimsy Farmer) and rustle up the money for tuition.

programme.desc: In this revered Western, Ethan Edwards (John Wayne) returns home to Texas after the Civil War. When members of his brother's family are killed or abducted by Comanches, he vows to track down his surviving relatives and bring them home. Eventually, Edwards gets word that his niece Debbie (Natalie Wood) is alive, and, along with her adopted brother, Martin Pawley (Jeffrey Hunter), he embarks on a dangerous mission to find her, journeying deep into Comanche territory.

programme.title: Mountain Lakes, United States

programme.title: Carl's Big Adventure - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Carl is visited by three large, very dirty construction vehicles. But Carl’s Car Wash is not big enough to fit them! Carl has an idea! He can supersize his car wash so the vehicles can get a proper scrub-a-dub-dub. He just needs a few parts. With the help of old friends, Carl sets out on a big adventure to the darkest depths of the ocean, the highest heights in the land, and beyond! Don't miss Carl in his first mini-movie as he pushes himself to the limit in the name of vehicular cleanliness!

programme.title: Where Is Baby? - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Peekaboo! Sing along with this adorable song for kids, “Where Is Baby?” Where is baby? Where is baby? Where is baby? There he is.

programme.title: Finny's First Day Of School - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Finny is a bit nervous on the first day of school, but Myrtle the Turtle is even more anxious and literally hides her shell! Surely Myrtle will come out of her shell when their teacher, a whale named Mr. Magellan, has them draw and present their favorite things to the class! Nope. Myrtle is even more nervous than ever! Being a good friend, Finny helps Myrtle through her anxiety, and the tiny turtle finally shows her drawing to everyone.

programme.title: Baby Shark - Finny The Shark and more
programme.desc: Baby Shark, Mama Shark, Papa Shark, Grandma Shark, and Grandpa Shark are out for a family swim and Baby Shark tries and tries to catch the little fishies but his skills aren’t yet up to the task. Just when he thinks he finally has them a (very big) friend of the little fishies steps in to save the day!

programme.title: Dharma's Digger - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Oh no! While digging deep at a top secret construction site, Dhama’s digger gets splashed with smelly sludge! Everyone says that Carl's Car Wash is the place to get all cleaned up, so Dharma skedaddles over to see how Carl can help. With a quick mess assessment and a few twists of some knobs, Carl’s suds-sational car wash is ready to give Dharma’s vehicle a good scrub-a-dub! Dharma rolls out one happy customer, and ready to get back to work on the site that Carl is itching to see!

programme.title: Mr. Golden Sun - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Mister Sun, Sun, Mr. Golden Sun...please shine down on me! It’s the Mr. Sun song from Super Simple Songs!

programme.title: Finny's First Sleepover - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: It’s Finny’s first sleepover and Finny is nervous to sleep at Myrtle the Turtle’s house. Throughout the night, Finny becomes more and more comfortable and after a spooky story, Finny becomes the bravest of everyone at the sleepover! Finny can't wait to do a sleepover again – but this time, at Finny’s house!

programme.title: When I Grow Up - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: What do you want to be when you grow up? Maybe a firefighter? Or an astronaut? Or maybe a dancer? Practice talking about jobs and careers with this super simple and super fun song, “When I Grow Up”.

programme.title: Enchanted Ice Cream - Super Simple
programme.desc: Today, the Bumble Nums are going to make Enchanted Ice Cream Sundaes! With a map in hand, Humble, Grumble, and Stumble search the orchard for the secret ingredient … and discover a fairy treehouse leading to an enchanted world! The Bumble Nums travel through the portal…but do they find the ingredient they’re looking for? And can they get it back to the kitchen in time for the Cooking Countdown? Watch and see!

programme.title: Hello Hello! - Super Simple Puppets and more
programme.desc: Sing and dance along with the Super Simple Puppets to “Hello Hello!”! It’s a classic Super Simple action song for kids.

programme.title: Down By The Bay - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Did you ever see cat wearing a hat? How about a goat rowing a boat? Well you can in this video for the classic children’s song, Down By The Bay!

programme.title: Erupting Raspberry Lava Cake - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: The Bumble Nums are making an Erupting Raspberry Lava Cake but the secret ingredient is at the top of Mount Fruity Volcano! Can Humble, Grumble, and Stumble climb the volcano and get what they need before it blows? And can they do it in time for the Cooking Countdown? Watch and see!

programme.title: Finny The Shark - Down In The Bay and more
programme.desc: Did you ever see a shark glowing in the dark? Did you ever see a stingray dancing at the ballet? Finny and Mama Shark are singing a rhyming song about all of their silly friends down in the bay! Sing along!

programme.title: Let's Go For A Drive! - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Get in on the fun and join Milo in his pretend car for a quick ride. Do you see the ducks crossing the road? How about people walking on the sidewalk? Wait, don't cross on a red light!

programme.title: Mr. Pig & Mr. Mouse Have A Mess On Their Hands - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Mr. Mouse and Mr. Pig love making food and having their friends over to eat it. But afterwards, their house is a complete mess! It's so gross, they don't even want to drive their van home to clean up. How can Mr. Mouse and Mr. Pig share their love of food with their friends without cluttering their home? Mr. Monkey has a tasty idea! What is it? Watch and find out!

programme.title: The Pinocchio - Sing Along With Tobee - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Caitie made a fluffy bird marionette named Fifi! Fifi is full of jokes and Tobee can't stop laughing! Caitie knows another marionette who is just as silly as Fifi. It's Pinocchio! There's even a whole song about him called "The Pinocchio." After Caitie and Tobee sing and dance to the song, Fifi can't help but tell one last joke. And guess what? It quacks Tobee up!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: For Children's Day, Wang Min's Dad has invited his son and his classmates to visit his toy factory. While cleaning up before the visit, a robot toy goes on a rampage. The Super Pets encounter an itchy lion at a sanctuary in South Africa. Jett and Astra deliver a back scratcher, but the lion's itchiness is stronger than they realized!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets go into a video game arcade and play a video game called Dessert Kingdom. They are watched by a boy named Yoonho and they decide to go into the game itself. When the Super Pets unearth a woolly mammoth, a Nenets boy wants to keep it. The mammoth must be able to pull a sleigh so Jett and Dizzy deliver a mammoth size sleigh.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: When a T-Rex skeleton on display in a Copenhagen museum is brought to life, a boy orders a remote controller to summon a giant robot T-Rex that he built to help calm it. Jett and the others arrive in the Jurassic Period to return Tyranno to her proper place in time. A volcano erupts throwing fireballs everywhere and causing a dinosaur stampede.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets spend a day at a Pet Hotel in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. The daughter of the hotel owner gets help from the Super Wings to keep them well behaved. After the Jet Pets' Mini Base collides with a small comet, Jett and Astra rush a space scanner to a young boy and together and the three search space for the small 'Baba' comet.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: It's Jett's birthday and almost all of his friends are throwing him a big birthday party! Sky sends Donnie, Paul, and Dizzy out on missions and nobody is left at Jett's party. When the truck transporting race cars gets lost, Jett, Jerome and Dizzy show up in Monza, Italy to take part in a very special Super Wings race against Golden Boy.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: While in Thailand, Jett Pet and Paul Pet's fruit is swiped by a monkey. Thanawat asks for help getting his toys back so they create an LED ball with a tracking system. The Super Pets travel to Madrid, Spain where they help a boy secure a case for his pet tarantula to ride the train. However an accident causes his tarantula to become super-sized!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: After eating Super Pets' trick chocolates, Jett can only move very slowly. An emergency call comes in from a young girl in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, who needs an ice maker delivered. When the Super Pets discover an erupting underwater volcano, a girl orders a megaphone to alert all the villagers fishing in the water. However, they don't listen to her warnings.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets are on World Aircraft to help Jett give some kids a tour. After Storm demonstrates how the package printer works, two packages get mixed up. The Super Pets travel to Tokyo, Japan where they help the Paper Rangers repair an Origami Robot for an action movie. Afterward, they get asked to be ACTORS in the movie!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets encounter mermaid students learning to befriend fish in Cebu, Philippines. Jett and Jerome deliver a microphone and speaker to a girl who wants to sing with them. The Super Pets meet a girl in Paris whose greatest wish is to go to the top of the Eiffel Tower. The elevator cannot run due to an expected storm so Jett and Donnie deliver a tram.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Storm and Stella search for Jett and Donnie after the two Super Wings disappear in thin air. Astra locates their whereabouts - they're in Paris, 1889! While exploring Osaka, Japan, the Super Pets accidentally rip a boy's koinobori for the Children's Day Festival. Jett and Dizzy deliver a huge koinobori for him.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets meet a boy and his giant robot on Jeju Island in South Korea. Before a typhoon comes, Taehoon needs to help his father harvest all the tangerines on their farm. When a little girl and her Dad accidentally fall in an Incan secret dark room, Jett, and Ellie show up in Machu Picchu, Peru, to take part in a historical rescue mission.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: When the Super Pets arrive at Ha Long, Vietnam, they meet a boy who wants to take his pet snail on a walk but the snail is too slow. So the boy asks for a snail size skateboard. The Super Pets go to Germany where they participate in a water fight. They accidentally shoot Mona's necklace. Jett and Astra deliver a magnifying glass to find the missing bead.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: When the Super Pets accidentally break a boulder on the roof of a house in Monsanto, Portugal, a girl orders tape to stick it back together. Golden Boy tries to outshine them. The Super Pets' Mini Base accidentally gets tangled up in an octopus kite, pulling it to the ground and destroying it. The Super Wings deliver paper and string to make a new kite.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Marty, with their new electric powers, save an amusement park in Germany. Jett and Dizzy also rescue a boat, an eel, and fix a jaguar chase, making everyone happy.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: In Singapore, Jett and Lucie use electric powers to shrink Happy the giant robot dog. In Paris, Jett and Marty turn a giant baguette into a train to deliver it to a festival.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Traver time-travel to uncover a dinosaur footprint’s creator. Jett and Dizzy rescue a bus stuck in a ditch using Monster Truck Tires, getting the kids to school on time.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett, Tiki, and Taki recover stolen costumes to save a dragon dance in Sichuan. In Antalya, Jett and Marty protect a giant sandcastle after Golden Girl's magic enlarges it and Jett

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As babies grow and change, so does the landscape around them. From fiery red leaves to falling snowflakes; from flowers in bloom to fireworks in July, every season of the year has its own special magic just waiting to be discovered.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Get moving with Baby Einstein! Real, live-action footage and classical melodies engage young viewers as they discover simple words related to movement. Enjoy encouraging melodies and live-action footage of babies and animals as you learn and move alongside your favorite Baby Einstein friends. Dance along as you watch real, live action footage of animals, children, and toys.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Learn about the numbers 1,2,3,4 & 5 through rhyme, real-life footage, counting, stories, and repetition - accompanied by classical music and real world imagery.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Set off on a fun-filled "field trip" with your baby to discover animals found at the park, on the farm, and in your own backyard.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: At 12 months, babies can identify specific sounds, and they re also starting to match the sounds they hear with the objects that make them. Meet the Orchestra -- First Instruments presents your little "maestro" with a playful introduction to the sights and sounds of a real orchestra -- from the clashing of cymbals to the purring of the cello and everything in between!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As each day passes, babies' awareness of the world around them -- and all the wonders it holds -- continues to develop. Wild animals are especially intriguing and World Animals captures the spirtit of little ones' fascination with wild creatures as it introduces them to animals living in the jungle, in the ocean, and on the savannah.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Ocean Explorers
programme.desc: Cal, Neptune, Opus, Dean, Tinker and others learn about the flora and fauna of the ocean through STEAM-based principles and real-life footage.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: One of the greatest joys you'll ever experience is bonding with your baby during those calm, quiet moments. Lullaby Time: Soothing Sounds for Baby provides comforting sights and sounds as you relax and share this precious downtime.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Meet fascinating creatures from across the globe and learn to run, hop, and crawl like your favorite animal friends. Dance and sing along to discover animal names and the native habitats of the world's most unique creatures. See real-life images of lions and zebras on the African savannah, then learn animal names through repetition and narration, or simply watch your favorite animals play!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As babies turn one year old, they continue to develop their communication skills. Meanwhile, with you as their guide, they also begin to explore the world beyond their front door. Baby's Favorite Places presents them with a playful introduction to 20 words for the names of familiar locations from the market, to the library, to the local park.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones' curiosity about the world blossoms, you can help sow the seeds of discovery with Baby MacDonald: A Day on the Farm. Featuring live-action images, nursery rhymes and fun-filled puppet shows, this playful, interactive program introduces little ones to the exciting sights and sounds of a real working farm.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: From the day their eyes open, babies begin to observe new things around the world and about themselves. Baby Da Vinci From Head to Toe presents them with a playful and interactive introduction to the human body in three languages -- English, Spanish, and French. With a whimsical blend of adorable puppets, real children and animals and famous works of art, this captivating program also features upbeat classical music.

programme.title: Dinobees Funny Cartoon Show for Kids
programme.desc: Welcome to #Dinobees, a funny show for kids. It revolves around a family that discovers and adopts three dinosaurs. This comedy cartoon is perfect entertainment for toddlers.Dinobees has some amazing 3d animations. Get ready to have great jungle adventures with cartoon animals and dinosaurs.This entertaining playlist is a collection of videos for children like Fire Discovery, Fruits of Labour, Magical Berries, Catch me If You can, Glue Trap, Mis Splits, I Wanna Fly, The Gophers, Baby Sitter, Frozen Lake, Bubble Trouble, Where's My Water and many more videos.

programme.title: Super Supremes Dinosaur Song & More Kids Songs
programme.desc: The kids are so excited to learn about dinosaurs. Here is a collection of our best videos.

programme.title: Super Car Royce - Back To Back
programme.desc: Watch this compilation from Super Car Royce Back To School!

programme.title: Little Red Car - Popular Songs
programme.desc: Watch this whole bunch of popular songs by the Little Red Car for kids. Hope you enjoy them!

programme.title: Humpty Dumpty Nursery Rhymes and Kids Songs - Farmees
programme.desc: Enjoy Farmees Humpty Dumpty and popular easy to learn nursery rhymes for babies and toddlers, which the children love to sing and dance along with. Learn these kids songs and new words at the same time.

programme.title: Luke and Lily Baby Shark - Shark Song For Kids
programme.desc: Kids! We've taken your favorite songs like Baby Shark, The Muffin Man, Little Bo Peep and put them in a compilation for several minutes of pure, uninterrupted fun learning and singing with Luke and Lily.

programme.title: Super Supremes Old Macdonald & More Kids Songs
programme.desc: Kids! Are you ready to go round the Farmees town? Hop on-board this Red Wheels On The Bus to enjoy your fun learning ride.

programme.title: Daddy's Red Car + Fun Nursery Rhymes for Kindergarten
programme.desc: Kids songs and nursery rhymes that your little one will love! Come join Super Supremes as they sing and dance in this fun kids video. Learn preschool songs, numbers, abc, shapes, colors and more made easy and fun with this nursery rhyme and kids song playlist with rhymes like Daddy’s red car, I spy wheels on the bus, Twinkle Twinkle Little Star and more.

programme.title: Humpty Dumpy & More Kids Rhymes (Little Treehouse)
programme.desc: Do you kids know what happened to Humpty Dumpty when he sat on the wall? Learn this classic nursery rhyme with the Little Tree House gang, plus many other fun songs and dances.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Robotnik invents a Brain Evolution Achievement Negatizer, or B.E.A.N., in order to make all of Mobius stupid. But instead of turning Sonic and Tails into dummies, the BEAN device turns them into three year old babies!

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Robotnik plans to block out the sun and extinguish all life and nature on the planet Mobius. Then he creates a robot son and tries to train him in the ways of wickedness.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Robotnik invents an electron altering generator which makes Scratch and Grounder twice as fast as Sonic. When they lose it and Tails picks it up, Tails speeds through the Mobius countryside leaving disasters and accidents in his wake.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Momma escapes again from the Mobius Home for Really Bizarre People, disowns Robotnik for failing to capture Sonic, then with the help of an obscure Mobius law, adopts Sonic!

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: When S and G rocket to outerspace to mine a radioactive mineral from asteroids, Sonic and Tails stow away. Through a blunder by the robots, all are stranded in space, and Robotnik tries to blow all of them out of the stars.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: When a Rebel Base is threatened by Robotnik, Sonic and Tails team up with Big Mike and Da Bears to run a booby-trapped gauntlet in their gigantic Road Warrior-like truck.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Robotnik pretends to retire, and lures Sonic and Tails into a phony vacation resort, laden with booby-traps set by Scratch and Grounder. But Sonic takes the bungee jumping, hang gliding, and skydiving in stride, and gives the bashes the bots.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: In order to free Sketch Lampoon (a satirical comic book artist) from Robotnik's new high security prison, Sonic allows himself to be captured, then makes a high speed rescue and escape.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: When Tails gets trapped in the "Warp of Confusion" (an Escher-like world of another dimension) by Robotnik's cousin, Dr. Warpnik, Sonic comes to the rescue and traps Robotnik and his badniks in the warp.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Robotnik's momma escapes from the home for mothers of villains and demands her birthday present! When she endangers Sonic and Tails, and they manage to have her taken back to the home, even Robotnik is glad to see her go.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Coconuts is exiled by Robotnik to a remote jungle, where he uses a giant baby ape to destroy villages. But Sonic exposes Coconuts as a robot, and the baby ape's real Dad takes Coconuts and the baby ape away for a spanking.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Outraged by a new hit song written about Sonic, Robotnik outlaws music on Mobius then takes pop star, Catty Carlisle captive. With his MD (Music Destroyer) Robot, Robotnik terrorizes the music loving Mobius population till Sonic rescues Catty.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: A fast talking, travelling salesman named Wes Weasley sells Robotnik various hedgehog-catching gadgets, including the Darkenator, the Super-duper Gravity Stopper, and the De-Atomizer.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Sonic rescues Lucas, a rival of Robotnik's, from Robotnik's dungeons. But Robotnik will do anything to keep Lucas from finding his lost love, Lucinda, including proposing to her himself.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Tails gets a megadose of Robotniks growth gas and grows quickly to monstrous proportions, eating everything in sight and accidentally terrorizing the countryside.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling takes Rainbow Ruby to Ellie's cabin where she meets mischievous baby stars that fell from the sky. The baby stars were playing together, got separated from their mother and ended up in Rainbow Village. // Kiki is extremely upset because the jewel Prince Frederick gave her as a gift broke. The friends try to glue the broken pieces together but it doesn't work. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a jewelry designer and tries designing jewelry with the broken pieces.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ruby is looking at shoes that are now too small for her but she is unwilling to throw away. Ling Ling is worried about Thunderbell. His scooter broke so he is not able to carry out his work as a mail carrier. // Gina calls Rainbow Ruby because today is the day to show their appreciation to Mayor Ling Ling. It took a long time for the village friends to prepare an amazing painting for the mayor. Unfortunately, Thunderbell accidentally destroyed the painting while delivering it.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: There is a new menu at the restaurant, which is very popular. There is a long line in front of the restaurant but there weren't enough staff members to serve the villagers the food. Rainbow Ruby turns into a waitress using her magic suitcase and serve tables kindly. // Rainbow Village is full of marbles. The marbles that Rainbow Ruby spilled in her room poured down into the village from up above the sky! Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to transform into a paramedic.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Gina, Thunderbell, and Choco try to cheer Jessy up but their efforts are of no use. While talking to Mayor Ling Ling, Rainbow Ruby realizes that today is the day that Jessy first arrived in Rainbow Village! Jessy was upset that her friends forgot such a meaningful day to her. // Ling Ling has caught a bad cold and was sneezing all over the place. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase and transforms into a doctor and cares for him.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried about the play planned for tonight. There are no costumes ready for the villagers so it's hard to figure out which roles they're playing. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a costume designer and designs costumes for her friends. // It is very windy in Rainbow village and Paige, a paper doll, is flying to wherever the wind takes her. She is so light that she is blown away by the wind. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a fire fighter and rescue Paige.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried that the villagers are all in low spirits. Ruby thinks of fun games to play but the villagers say that they have already played all of the games too many times. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a singer. // Ling Ling is worried because of the "Secret Present Swapping Party" that Thunderbell is preparing. Thunderbell forgot to send out the invitations. Rainbow Ruby turns into a mail carrier and delivers the invitations.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that a Rainbow Ball Tournament is about to start soon but he is worried because the field is covered with pokey pods. Then Ellie tells her she lost her needles, Gina her hat, and Thunderbell his wheels. Who could have taken away all these things?

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling is waiting for Rainbow Ruby, looking worried. It turns out that Mr. Sloth's best friend Chirpee is depressed. Chirpee wants to fly high but is struggling due to his small wings and lack of means to learn how to fly. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that the dress that the paper doll Paige was planning to wear to Kiki's dance party is torn. Paige very much wants to go to the party but she cannot go without a dress.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: In Rainbow Village, people are busy preparing for a dance marathon, a competition in which all of the villagers participate. Ling Ling is very sad that Ellie decided not to participate. Ellie is not a confident dancer so she doesn't want to participate. // Rainbow Ruby's friends tell her that they are now friends with Felicia. They were playing hide and seek but Felicia is such a good hider that she's refused to come out even though she's won the game.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Kiki wants to become a movie actress but the villagers have no idea how to direct a movie. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a movie director. She then assigns roles to the village friends. // Ling Ling rushes toward Rainbow Ruby to tell her that a busy villager mom asked him to take care of her three children but he just doesn't have time to look after them. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to transform into a babysitter and starts babysitting the children.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Out gathering berries one day, Strawberry & her pets find a tiny fairy among the berry vines. Realizing that Strawberry is a very kind & caring person, the little fairy decides to take advantage of the kid's good nature.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: One windy day, a tiny fairy named Sherry Bobbleberry gets blown into Strawberry's open window. She is a klutzy little thing who is convinced she will always be a major goof-up.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Apple Dumplin' wants to help Strawberry & her Friends with the baking. But Dumplin's just a little too little to be much help. In fact, she ends up making a pretty big mess of things.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: The Berry Blossom Festival is underway and everyone in Strawberryland is having a Grand Time, thanks to the selfless efforts of Strawberry Shortcake: she's really giving this festival all she has!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry and her friends love to dance - and the Peculiar Purple Pieman of Porcupine Peak is taking advantage of that!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: It's winter and the pond's frozen and that means Strawberry and friends can ice skate! They decide to showcase their talents at an Ice Dance Show. But just then, a new kid named Apricot shows up.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry and her friends are getting the band back together to perform at a local festival, but Ginger Snap is out of town.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake is planning a first birthday party for her sister Apple Dumpling, so she sets off on an adventure to find the perfect party supplies.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake travels to magical Holidayland with Honey Pie Pony, searching for the perfect presents to give her friends.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: It's the first day of spring in Strawberryland, at least it's supposed to be, but with the cold winds blowing and frost on the ground it looks as though spring isn't going to come this year.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Honey Pie Pony hurts her leg and must stay in bed instead of heading off for a grand adventure. A disappointed Honey proves to be a very impatient patient, that is until Strawberry Shortcake and her friends work together telling stories and...

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Honey Pie Pony is feeling left out of all the kids' play. She's the only one with four legs. There's no one like her to talk to and share her feelings. So Strawberry and the others take Honey Pie to Ice Cream Island - a land of horses.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas looks forward to fireworks and uses his new whistle to make engines jump.

programme.desc: Fearless Freddie challenges Rheneas and Skarloey to a race and Thomas fixes Toby’s shed.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: James gets a new coat of paint, the engines enter a competition and Thomas receives help.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: A special statue is delivered, Toby’s windmill is damaged and Henry collects a flagpole.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: James gets an important job, Thomas wants a medal and Thomas takes three specials.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas shunts trucks while James collects flour and Sir Handle transports apples.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: The engines keep a secret, James collects the Queen of Sodor and Emily gets a new route.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas meets “Hector the Horrid” and Gordon takes an engineer to repair some points.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas meets Jeremy, a jet plane, and a carnival comes to the island.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas wants new trucks, lambs get stuck and The Narrow Guage engines carry coal.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: The little narrow gauge engines bring sheep to safety. Then, Duncan finds a new job.

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Skarloey doesn’t like the Wharf and Thomas collects timber for Farmer McColl’s roof.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: After feeling tired of being told to be careful, BJ learns that saying be careful is a way of showing love and concern. This important lesson prompts Barney to teach his friends essential safety rules. Spending time with Barney is always enjoyable, but it's also crucial to learn how to play it safe to stay protected and happy!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney and his friends begin the day by playing hopscotchwith a twist. They discover that making a triangle requires 3 lines and 3 corners, which sparks their excitement to explore more shapes. To have even more triangle fun, Barney takes them on a camping trip where they get to explore and learn about different shapes in the world around them.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Its the day of the bike parade, but Nick has had a bike accident that leaves his bike in need of repair by Mr. Boyd. Mr. Boyd works quickly to help fix Nick's bike in time for the parade. While waiting, Barney takes the opportunity to teach his friends all about bike safety, making sure they're ready to ride safely and have a great time in the parade!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: After the kids discuss their desire for a special day, Beth creates Happy Day, a special holiday in which each friend makes a wish and the others try to make it come true. When Beth hurts herself, BJ learns the importance of doing good deeds for others.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney plans a magical picnic for his friends and their families to spend quality time together and create lasting memories. Each kid has brought a family treasure to show Barney, which they all excitedly spend time sharing with one another.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: The kids gather at the park, talking about how hot it is and wondering what to do on such a day. Thanks to Barney's brilliant idea, what starts as a too hot afternoon turns into a totally cool day as he introduces them to the wonders of water. Excitedly, they all team up to find creative ways to stay cool and have a splashing good time together!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Even though the park feels like a second home for Barney and his friends, BJ dreams of building his very own totally awesome, completely cool house. While they eagerly wait to see it, they start talking about the different rooms a house can have. However, when BJ forgets to include a space for Baby Bop in his plans, Barney reminds him that family is what truly makes a house feel like a home.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: After watching birds fly overhead, Riff begins to daydream of flying. He decides to build his very own rocket ship and fly to Mars. The flight is not exactly a success, but Barney encourages Riff to never give up on his dream. After seeing many moms playing with their kids in the park, Baby Bop is curious as to what makes somebody a mommy. With Barney's help, Baby Bop learns all about what makes mommies so special.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop's blankey seems to be getting smaller; she's convinced it's shrinking. But Barney shows her that, actually, Baby Bop is growing. When Riff takes a bite of ice cream, he realizes has a very sore tooth. Afraid of going to the dentist, Barney and his friends put on a show to help Riff see there is nothing to be afraid of - the dentist is his friend.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Marcos accidentally breaks an art show project that Melanie has made. Afraid that she will be angry, he doesn't say anything, but then feels guilty. Barney helps him see that honesty is the best policy. Meanwhile, Myra, has decided she doesn't like her name. After learning about many names and what they mean, Barney helps Myra see how special her name truly is.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Melanie is sad because she misses her friend, Marisa who is visiting relatives in India. Barney takes her and the other kids on a trip to the magic caboose to visit pretend countries like Egypt, Tahiti, and India. When they were in a pretend country of India, they saw a real elephant and in the end, Melanie gets a email from her friend in India (who has also seen a real elephant in India!). BJ wants to be a great magician, but he doesn't want to spend any time practicing. He convinces Baby Bop to help him do a trick, and makes her disappear. When BJ thought she is gone forever, he realizes the importance of practice. Luckily, Baby Bop hasn't disappeared after all!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney and his friends are decorating the caboose for Christmas when Barney decides to plan a Secret Santa game. Everyone has to buy or make a gift for the friend whose name they have drawn. Melanie misses the mailman, so Barney takes her to Santa's workshop at the North Pole to deliver her wish list in person.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Riff decides to create a musical zoo when their zoo trip is postponed. Gathering instruments that sound like real animals, Riff gives Barney and his friends a zoo-mazing experience. When Baby Bop hesitates on keeping her promise to watch Ryan's pet frog, Barney tells her the story of The Frog Prince. And Baby Bop learns about keeping her promises.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: When Baby Bop finds out that Daniel is going on a trip the next day, she wants to go on a trip as well. Everyone decides to pretend to travel the same way Daniel will travel. First, they all make cardboard cars out of some scrap boxes. Next, everyone pretends to be an airplane. Then Barney turns the cardboard airplanes into a great big cardboard bus. They go on a camera safari only to discover the last mode of their transportation--a big cardboard cruise ship.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: After noticing parts of the moon are missing in the night sky, Baby Bop is determined to find the rest of it. It's up to Barney and the kids to help Baby Bop take a trip to outer space where they meet Mr. Moon. Baby Bop then learns that the moon is not always full at night.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Who is buried under Glasgow Cathedral and why? Who is Saint Mungo, and why is he so big? Why does Glasgow City Chambers look like a palace? How did this seaport town become a center for Scottish Enlightenment? And how did the creativity of one man, Charles Rennie Mackintosh, leave a permanent mark on this city?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why did the English choose Jamestown, Yorktown and Williamsburg, Virginia as their early American settlements? Why did King William III and Queen Mary II establish a university here? What was life like for the colonists here? What crazy concoctions are to be found in a colonial apothecary?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: After nearly 500 years, French is still the official language of this North American city. How has this UNESCO World Heritage Site retained its Frenchness after all this time? Why does the Chteau Frontenac hotel look like a French castle, and what does it have to do with the Canadian railway? Whats so curious about Rue Saint-Jean, Place Royal and Petit-Champlain?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Englands Great West Way follows the ancient path from London to Bristol. We take the train & get curious at stops along the way: Stonehenge, Baths masonic architecture, the Roman Baths, the S.S. Great Britain, sample some curious pub pies, learn some cockney rhyming slang, and visit some charming villages too.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is La Grande-Place so gilded? How did Brussels chocolate legacy begin? Where can you walk through the pages of a comic book? How did Flanders become such a source of Master artists?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is there a giant wall in the middle of Cardiff? Who built Cardiff Castle? And Coch Castle? Why does Cardiff have so many Victorian and Georgian arcades? What is a bara brith? And laverbread? (Seriously, what is it?). Where did Princess Diana give her first public speech in Welsh?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: How did Manchester kick-start Railway Mania in the Industrial Age? What is Manchesters Water Palace? What is so curious about the architecture of the John Rylands and Chethams Libraries? Why is a bee the symbol of Manchester? Which pub owner shooed her patrons out with a broom? Why is the National Football Museum in Manchester?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: How did Hong Kong get its name? What is so junk-y about a junk boat? Get curious about the Big Buddha, the Po Lin Monastery, and the Wisdom Path on Lantau Island. Then, the Kung Wo Tofu Factory, to learn the centuries-old tofu-making technique. We also take you inside a Tin Hau Temple, the Man Mo Temple, and a traditional Qipao dress-making shop.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: We get curious about the stunning natural landscape of Wales. We visit Brecon Beacons National Park, Pembrokeshire National Park, St. Davids (the smallest city in Britain), Hay-onWye (WalesTown of Books), the Victorian seaside resort town of Tenby, and lots and lots of curious castles.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why did the English choose Jamestown, Yorktown and Williamsburg, Virginia as their early American settlements? Why did King William III and Queen Mary II establish a university here? What was life like for the colonists here? What crazy concoctions are to be found in a colonial apothecary?
